page: Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is a shared-allocation virtual machine within the Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with 65 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU score is 1260.88, and the multi-core score is 2444.49, both categorized in the bottom 25 percent of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only small block cached memory reaching 177.44 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds drop to 12.18 GB/sec and 10.85 GB/sec respectively. PassMark benchmarks reflect similar trends, with a CPU score of 2453.98 and a database operations score of 386.17, both in the bottom 10 percent.

In application-specific tests, the instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 1.72 GB/sec and handles 1.39 million Redis SET operations per second. For lightweight machine learning tasks, prompt processing on a small 135M parameter model reaches 115.73 tokens per second, with text generation at 32.64 tokens per second. Given the shared CPU allocation and modest resource density, this server offers a highly cost-efficient profile for low-intensity workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight background tasks, and small utility databases.
description: Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is a shared-allocation virtual machine in the Cloud Compute family, featuring an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is configured with 2.0 GB of RAM and 65 GB of NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2444.49 and a PassMark CPU score of 2453.98, placing it in the lower performance tiers. Large-block uncached memory write speeds reach 10.85 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 1.72 GB/sec. This server provides a budget-friendly, entry-level resource profile with shared CPU allocation, making it suitable for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development testing, and small utility applications.
